name: Ribadeo – tour around Ría de Foz – Special Conservation Area Ría de Foz-Masma
name_original: Ribadeo – vuelta por Ría de Foz – Zona de Especial Conservación Ría de Foz-Masma
region_intro: Touring cycling around Barreiros, Spain, offers a diverse landscape characterized by its extensive coastline, green valleys, and protected natural spaces. The region features 9 kilometers of open sea coastline with fine, white sand beaches, alongside inland areas with rivers and forests. Cyclists can navigate varied terrain, from coastal promenades to routes with challenging ascents towards panoramic viewpoints. This blend of coastal and inland features provides a range of experiences for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Barreiros The most popular touring cycling route is Ribadeo – tour around Ría de Foz – Special Conservation Area Ría de Foz-Masma, a 29.2 miles (46.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours to complete, offering views of the Ría de Foz and a protected natural area. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is A Rapadoira Beach – Ría de Foz loop from Reinante, a moderate 24.8 miles (39.9 km) path. This route features coastal stretches and views of the Ría de Foz estuary. Local touring cyclists also love the Beach of the Cathedrals – Rinlo ️ loop from Barreiros, a 25.2 miles (40.6 km) trail leading through coastal areas and charming towns, often completed in about 2 hours 33 minutes. Touring cycling around Barreiros is defined by its stunning coastline, green valleys, and panoramic viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from gentle coastal rides to more demanding inland ascents. The routes in Barreiros are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 1000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Barreiros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Barreiros?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes around Barreiros, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 6 easy, 6 moderate, and 12 difficult routes, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Barreiros?
A: Barreiros offers a varied landscape for touring cyclists. You'll find routes along its 9 kilometers of stunning coastline with fine, white sand beaches, as well as inland paths through green valleys, forests, and along rivers. Some routes feature challenging ascents towards panoramic viewpoints, while others are gentler stretches along the coast or through picturesque towns.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Barreiros?
A: Yes, Barreiros features several circular touring cycling routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14987300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Rapadoira Beach – Ría de Foz loop from Reinante&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 24.8-mile (39.9 km) path with coastal stretches and views of the Ría de Foz estuary.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features I can see along the cycling routes?
A: Cyclists can explore a wealth of natural and historical landmarks. Along the coast, you might encounter the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/526099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beach of the Cathedrals&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2833444" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia das Illas&lt;/a&gt;. Inland, the region boasts green valleys and even waterfalls, such as those found near Santo Estevo do Ermo. You can also visit historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/359296" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Salvador&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. The region is home to viewpoints like Mount Penabor and Mount Comado, which are classified as Galician Special Landscape Interest Areas. Routes that ascend to these points offer expansive views of the A Mariña Lucense coastline and even glimpses of Asturias.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Barreiros?
A: While cycling is enjoyable throughout much of the year, August is a particularly popular time for cyclists in Barreiros. The region's coastal climate generally offers pleasant conditions for exploring its diverse routes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling options?
A: Yes, some routes are suitable for families, especially those along the coast or through flatter inland areas. The extensive promenade of the 'Camino Natural del Cantábrico' provides an excellent, accessible viewpoint for the coast and is suitable for cycling with varying skill levels.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Barreiros?
A: The touring cycling routes in Barreiros are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 100 reviews. Cyclists often praise the stunning coastline, green valleys, and the variety of options available for different ability levels.

Q: Can I find routes that are part of historical trails?
A: Yes, sections of the North Santiago Way pass through the municipality, integrating cycling with cultural and historical exploration. An example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40322680" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Northern Way of the Camino de Santiago – Monastery of San Salvador loop from A Vilavella&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that combine coastal views with charming towns?
A: Yes, many routes offer this combination. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31917717" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beach of the Cathedrals – Rinlo 🌉🏘️ loop from Barreiros&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 25.2-mile (40.6 km) trail that leads through coastal areas and charming towns like Rinlo.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Barreiros offers routes with more punishing profiles and significant ascents. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40322954" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rinlo 🌉🏘️ – Fishing wall loop from Reinante&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 66.8-kilometer route with over 570 meters of elevation gain, providing a demanding experience.
tips:
In Foz, an old whaling port, the sea is transformed into a beach: A Rapadoira, Llas, Peizás, Arealonga... About 15 km of beaches of fine and white sand mark this estuary. In its marshes, one of the largest colonies of waterfowl in the north-west is sheltered, watched from above by gulls, shearwaters and gannets from the neighboring cliffs. The Ria de Foz is a natural paradise not only for birds, but also for lovers of water sports: surfing, windsurfing, kite flying, canoeing, sailing.
https://www.turismo.gal/que-visitar/rias-e-praias/ria-de-foz?langId=es_ES
Below the tall coastal cliffs stretches a wide sandy beach across the wide bay. Stairs lead down from the bike trail to the foot of the cliffs
The coastal trail is well paved and leads along the wide sandy beach high stop the cliffs plateau to the Ermita with several rest areas and short viewpoint trails branching off.
Right off the paved bike trail with benches, an ancient coastal wall closes off this small river estuary for fishing.

Ribadeo is located on the large bay of the river of the same name. In the sports harbor of the place you can eat excellent octopus. At the narrowest point of the bay, this is spanned by a motorway bridge. If you cross these you have a great view of the village and drive from Galicia to Asturias.

The representative buildings around the Plaza de Espana reflect the wealth of the coastal town on an excellent protected natural harbor, augmented by the wealth of the emigrants that returned after getting rich in South America.
